programs.title: America's Book of Secrets
programs.desc: In 1962, Martin Luther King Jr. criticized Hoover's FBI for refusing to prosecute white supremacists who had burned down Black churches in Albany, Ga.; Hoover took King's criticisms as a personal insult, and he ordered the FBI to investigate King.

programs.title: America's Book of Secrets
programs.desc: America's wealthiest citizens and how their power and influence take root.

programs.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programs.desc: From ancient religious texts buried in Egypt to cultural relics found at sea, or even a tower of skulls hiding under the earth, these lost artifacts change what is known about certain sacred beliefs.

programs.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programs.desc: Some secrets refuse to stay buried; whether it's illicit cargo in a frozen lake, a stolen masterpiece worth millions, or a brothel hidden in the shadow of the Capitol-the truth always resurfaces.

programs.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programs.desc: The world's greatest fortunes often surface in the most unlikely places. From a Fabergé egg found at a humble flea market, to rare baseball cards hidden inside an old piano, or even a backyard sapphire worth a king's ransom. These treasures prove that sometimes one person's forgotten clutter can become another's once-in-a-lifetime discovery.

programs.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programs.desc: A long-lost keepsake tied to the world's most famous astronaut, a giant flying reptile that once ruled the skies, and a previously unknown planet orbiting two suns; some extraordinary discoveries remind us to keep looking up.
